name: 03-qa-open-question
description: Question_asked state — speaker asks an open technical question (Postgres pgvector to Qdrant migration), follow-up provides scale context, then topic drifts. Tests the [answer, talking, fact] mix.

transcript.chunk: What's the right way to migrate from Postgres pgvector to a dedicated vector store like Qdrant — do you do it gradually or all at once?
We're at about 5 million vectors today, growing to maybe 50 million by end of year.
Anyway, separately — anyone seen Anthropic's new pricing for batch jobs?

classifier.state_evidence: Speaker explicitly asks 'do you do it gradually or all at once' about a Postgres-to-Qdrant migration. The follow-up about scale (5M to 50M vectors) provides context. Final chunk introduces an unrelated topic, but the migration question remains unanswered.
open_questions: gradual vs all-at-once Postgres-to-Qdrant migration approach

cards.preview: Gradual migration: dual-write to both stores, verify Qdrant matches Postgres on shadow queries, then cut reads per-feature.
cards.full_context: Standard migration pattern for stateful storage swaps. Dual-write isolates correctness verification from cutover risk; shadow reads against the new store let you measure parity before any user traffic depends on it.
grounded_in: [00:00] What's the right way to migrate from Postgres pgvector to a dedicated vector store like Qdrant
[00:30] We're at about 5 million vectors today, growing to maybe 50 million by end of year.

tuple.core_claim: dual-write plus shadow reads then per-feature cutover

cards.preview: 5M-to-50M growth crosses the inflection where pgvector index rebuilds start hurting (typically past 10M rows).
cards.full_context: pgvector's HNSW index becomes a maintenance liability past ~10M rows on commodity hardware: rebuilds take hours and can lock writes. The 5M-to-50M trajectory crosses that threshold mid-year.
grounded_in: [00:30] We're at about 5 million vectors today, growing to maybe 50 million by end of year.

tuple.core_claim: index rebuild times become operational pain past 10M rows

cards.preview: Verified: Qdrant supports hot-reload of HNSW index parameters during dual-write, no collection downtime needed.
cards.full_context: Qdrant's collection-update API allows changing HNSW parameters (m, ef_construct) without taking the collection offline. This matters for the dual-write phase because you can iterate on index tuning while migration is in progress.
grounded_in: [00:00] What's the right way to migrate from Postgres pgvector to a dedicated vector store like Qdrant

tuple.core_claim: hot-reload of HNSW params is supported in production
